source_story: As a Publisher, I want to publish a dataset, so that I can share the dataset publicly with everyone.
target_story: As a Publisher, I want to have private data packages, so that I can share just with my team.
label: NONE

source_story: As an Owner, I want to edit my profile, so that that it is updated with new information.
target_story: As an owner, I want to remove a user as an owner, so that they are just a member and no longer have full control.
label: NONE

source_story: As a publisher, I want to show the world how my published data is, so that that it immediately catches consumer's attention.
target_story: As a Publisher, I want to see real examples of published packages so that I can understand how useful and simple the datapackage format and the registry is.
label: NONE

source_story: As a Data Analyst, I want to update previously downloaded data package, so that that I can work with the most recent data.
target_story: As a Consumer, I want to know full details of how the data package schema has changed, so that that I can adjust my scripts to handle it.
label: NONE

source_story: As a Web Developer, I want to be able to install multiple versions of the same datapackage separately, so that that all my projects could be developed independently and deployed locally.
target_story: As a Publisher, I want to version my Data Package and keep multiple versions around including older versions, so that that I do not break consumer systems when I change my datapackage.
label: NONE

source_story: As a consumer, I want to view the data package, so that that I can get a sense of whether I want this dataset or not.
target_story: As a Consumer, I want to view a publisher's profile, so that that I can see who is behind a particular package or to see what other packages they produce.
label: NONE

source_story: As an owner, I want to make a user an owner, so that they have full control.
target_story: As an Admin, I want to have a pricing plan and billing system, so that that I can charge users and make my platform sustainable.
label: NONE

source_story: As a Publisher, I want to permanently delete a data package, so that that it no longer takes up storage space.
target_story: As a Publisher, I want to sign up for a given pricing plan, so that that I am entitled to what it allows.
label: NONE

source_story: As a Consumer, I want to download the data package in one file, so that that I don't have to download descriptor and each resource by hand.
target_story: As a Publisher, I want to be warned that a tag exists when I try to overwrite it, so that that I don't accidentally overwrite stable tagged data which is relied on by consumers.
label: NONE

source_story: As an Admin, I want to invite someone to join the platform, so that that they can start contributing or using data.
target_story: As a Publisher, I want to configure my client, so that I can start publishing data packages.
label: NONE

source_story: As a Consumer, I want to search data packages, so that that I can find the ones I want.
target_story: As a Consumer, I want to browse and find publishers, so that that I can find interesting publishers and their packages.

target_story: As a Consumer, I want to search data packages, so that that I can find the ones I want.

target_story: As a Consumer, I want to see how much the data has been downloaded, so that that I can choose most popular in the case when there are several alternatives for my use case.
label: NONE

target_story: As a Publisher, I want to have private data packages, so that I can share just with my team.

target_story: As a security administrator, I want visitors to be prohibited from signing up via GitHub or Google because new accounts may only be created through the standard email-and-password registration flow.

label: DUPLICATE

source_story: As a platform owner, I want Admins to be prohibited from inviting someone to join the platform because only platform owners may send invitations for new contributors or data users.
target_story: As an Admin, I want to invite someone to join the platform, so that that they can start contributing or using data.
label: CONFLICT

source_story: As an Admin, I want to invite someone to join the platform, so that that they can start contributing or using data.
target_story: As an Admin, I want to invite only people who already have an active platform account, not people who have not joined the platform, so that current users can start contributing or using data.
label: CONFLICT

source_story: As an Admin, I want to invite someone to join the platform, so that that they can start contributing or using data.
target_story: As an Admin, I want to configure the available user roles and data access groups so that new invitees can be assigned the right permissions when they join the platform.
label: DEPENDS_ON

source_story: As a Consumer, I want to see how much the data has been downloaded, so that that I can choose most popular in the case when there are several alternatives for my use case.
target_story: As a Consumer, I want to view how often the data has been downloaded, so that I can select the most popular option when multiple alternatives fit my use case.
label: DUPLICATE

source_story: As a Consumer, I want to see how much the data has been downloaded, so that that I can choose most popular in the case when there are several alternatives for my use case.
target_story: As a data catalog administrator, I want Consumers to be prohibited from seeing how many times data has been downloaded because only dataset owners may access download-count metrics.
label: CONFLICT

source_story: As a Consumer, I want to see how much the data has been downloaded, so that that I can choose most popular in the case when there are several alternatives for my use case.
target_story: As a Consumer, I want to see only when the data has not been downloaded at all, not how much it has already been downloaded, so that I can identify alternatives with no prior usage for my use case.
label: CONFLICT

source_story: As a Consumer, I want to see how much the data has been downloaded, so that that I can choose most popular in the case when there are several alternatives for my use case.
target_story: As a Platform Administrator, I want each dataset download to be recorded and linked to the correct dataset, so that reliable download counts are available for consumers.
label: DEPENDS_ON

source_story: As a Publisher, I want to see real examples of published packages so that I can understand how useful and simple the datapackage format and the registry is.
target_story: As a Publisher, I want to view actual examples of published packages so that I can understand how useful and easy to use the datapackage format and the registry are.
label: DUPLICATE

source_story: As a Publisher, I want to see real examples of published packages so that I can understand how useful and simple the datapackage format and the registry is.
target_story: As a registry administrator, I want Publishers to be denied access to real examples of published packages because only registry administrators may view example packages in the registry.
label: CONFLICT

source_story: As a Publisher, I want to see real examples of published packages so that I can understand how useful and simple the datapackage format and the registry is.
target_story: As a Publisher, I want to see real examples only of unpublished draft packages, not published packages, so that I can understand how useful and simple the datapackage format and the registry is before publication.
label: CONFLICT

source_story: As a Publisher, I want to see real examples of published packages so that I can understand how useful and simple the datapackage format and the registry is.
target_story: As a registry administrator, I want to seed the registry with approved public datapackage examples so that publishers have real published packages available to browse.
label: DEPENDS_ON

source_story: As a Consumer, I want to download the data package in one file, so that that I don't have to download descriptor and each resource by hand.
target_story: As a Consumer, I want to get the full data package as a single file, so that I do not need to manually download the descriptor and every resource separately.
label: DUPLICATE

source_story: As a Consumer, I want to download the data package in one file, so that that I don't have to download descriptor and each resource by hand.
target_story: As a data governance manager, I want Consumers to be prohibited from downloading a data package as one file because only data curators may export complete package bundles.
label: CONFLICT

source_story: As a Consumer, I want to download the data package in one file, so that that I don't have to download descriptor and each resource by hand.
target_story: As a Consumer, I want to download the data package as separate descriptor and resource files, not as one bundled file, so that I can handle each package component individually.
label: CONFLICT

source_story: As a Consumer, I want to download the data package in one file, so that that I don't have to download descriptor and each resource by hand.
target_story: As a Data Provider, I want to publish a data package with its descriptor and all associated resources included, so that consumers have a complete package available for single-file download.
label: NONE

source_story: As a Developer, I want to use data package as a node lib in my project, so that that I can depend on it using my normal dependency framework.
target_story: As a Developer, I want to include the data package as a Node library in my project, so that I can manage it with my normal dependency framework.
label: DUPLICATE

source_story: As a Developer, I want to use data package as a node lib in my project, so that that I can depend on it using my normal dependency framework.
target_story: As a platform maintainer, I want developers to be prohibited from using the data package as a node lib in their projects because the data package may only be accessed through the approved service API.
label: CONFLICT

source_story: As a Developer, I want to use data package as a node lib in my project, so that that I can depend on it using my normal dependency framework.
target_story: As a Developer, I want to use the data package in my project only as a static data package, not as a node lib, so that I manage it outside my normal dependency framework.
label: CONFLICT

source_story: As a Developer, I want to use data package as a node lib in my project, so that that I can depend on it using my normal dependency framework.
target_story: As a Developer, I want the data package to be published as a versioned Node package in the project package registry, so that I can install it through my normal dependency framework.

source_story: As a Consumer, I want to load a Data Package from R, so that that I can immediately start playing with it.

label: DUPLICATE

source_story: As a DataPackage owner, I want Consumers to be prohibited from downloading a DataPackage's data as one coherent SQLite database because only DataPackage owners may export the package in that form.

source_story: As a Consumer, I want to download a DataPackage's data one coherent SQLite database, so that that I can get it easily in one form.
target_story: As a Consumer, I want to download a DataPackage's data only as its separate original data files, not as one coherent SQLite database, so that I can work with each file in its native form.

source_story: As a Consumer, I want to download a DataPackage's data one coherent SQLite database, so that that I can get it easily in one form.
target_story: As a DataPackage maintainer, I want to publish the package with validated data resources and metadata so that it can be prepared as one coherent SQLite database for consumers.
label: DEPENDS_ON

target_story: As a Data Analyst, I want to locally compare different versions of a datapackage so that I can clearly identify schema changes and adapt my analytics code to the schema version I need.

source_story: As a Data Analyst, I want to compare different versions of some datapackage locally, so that that I can see schema changes clearly and adjust my analytics code to the desired schema version.
target_story: As a Data Governance Manager, I want Data Analysts to be prohibited from comparing different versions of a datapackage locally because only Data Stewards may perform datapackage schema version comparisons in the governed environment.

source_story: As a Data Analyst, I want to compare different versions of some datapackage locally, so that that I can see schema changes clearly and adjust my analytics code to the desired schema version.
target_story: As a Data Analyst, I want to compare different versions of a datapackage only from the remote registry, not from local copies, so that I can see schema changes clearly and adjust my analytics code to the desired schema version.

target_story: As a Web Developer, I want to install different versions of the same datapackage side by side, so that each of my projects can be developed independently and deployed locally.

source_story: As a Web Developer, I want to be able to install multiple versions of the same datapackage separately, so that that all my projects could be developed independently and deployed locally.
target_story: As a release manager, I want Web Developers to be prohibited from installing multiple versions of the same datapackage separately because each local deployment may use only the single approved version managed by release engineering.
label: CONFLICT

source_story: As a Web Developer, I want to be able to install multiple versions of the same datapackage separately, so that that all my projects could be developed independently and deployed locally.
target_story: As a Web Developer, I want only one version of the same datapackage to be installed locally at a time, so that all my projects use a consistent shared package version.

source_story: As a Web Developer, I want to be able to install multiple versions of the same datapackage separately, so that that all my projects could be developed independently and deployed locally.

label: DEPENDS_ON

source_story: As a Developer, I want to list all DataPackages requirements for my project in the file and pin the exact versions of any DataPackage that my project depends on, so that that the project can be deterministically deployed locally and won't break because of the DataPackage schema changes.
target_story: As a Developer, I want to specify every DataPackages requirement for my project in the file and lock each dependent DataPackage to an exact version, so that the project can be deployed locally in a deterministic way and is not broken by DataPackage schema changes.
label: DUPLICATE

source_story: As a release manager, I want developers to be prohibited from listing or pinning DataPackage requirements in project files because only release managers may define the approved DataPackage dependencies and exact versions for a project.
target_story: As a Developer, I want to list all DataPackages requirements for my project in the file and pin the exact versions of any DataPackage that my project depends on, so that that the project can be deterministically deployed locally and won't break because of the DataPackage schema changes.
label: CONFLICT

source_story: As a Developer, I want to list all DataPackages requirements for my project in the file and pin the exact versions of any DataPackage that my project depends on, so that that the project can be deterministically deployed locally and won't break because of the DataPackage schema changes.
target_story: As a Developer, I want to list all DataPackages requirements for my project in the file without pinning exact versions of any DataPackage that my project depends on, so that the project can accept compatible DataPackage schema updates during local deployment.
label: CONFLICT

source_story: As a Developer, I want to list all DataPackages requirements for my project in the file and pin the exact versions of any DataPackage that my project depends on, so that that the project can be deterministically deployed locally and won't break because of the DataPackage schema changes.
target_story: As a Developer, I want to view the available DataPackages with their published version history and schema details, so that I can identify the exact DataPackage versions my project depends on.

source_story: As a Publisher, I want to be able to get access to a previous version I tagged, so that that I can return to it and review it.
target_story: As a Publisher, I want to access an earlier version that I tagged so that I can go back to it and review it.
label: DUPLICATE

source_story: As a Publisher, I want to be able to get access to a previous version I tagged, so that that I can return to it and review it.
target_story: As a repository administrator, I want Publishers to be prohibited from accessing previous versions they tagged because only repository administrators may retrieve archived tagged versions for review.
label: CONFLICT

source_story: As a Publisher, I want to be able to get access to a previous version I tagged, so that that I can return to it and review it.
target_story: As a Publisher, I want to be able to get access to a previous version only while it is untagged, not after I have tagged it, so that I can return to and review versions that have not been marked yet.
label: CONFLICT

label: DEPENDS_ON

source_story: As a Consumer, I want to get a sense of the age of the datapackage that I have downloaded before, so that that I can decide if I should update or not.
target_story: As a Consumer, I want to see how old a datapackage I previously downloaded is, so that I can determine whether I should update it.

target_story: As a Consumer, I want to get a sense of the age only for a datapackage that I have not downloaded before, not for one already in my downloads, so that I can decide whether it is current enough to download.

source_story: As a Consumer, I want to get a sense of the age of the datapackage that I have downloaded before, so that that I can decide if I should update or not.
target_story: As a Consumer, I want the system to retain the download date and version information for datapackages I have downloaded, so that their age can be determined later.

source_story: As a Consumer, I want to see how active the site is, so that I can decide if I should get involved.
